nodes.label: P3.2 (side road A) — the depth-headroom confirmation (decisive)
nodes.description: Headroom hunt (scan_headroom.py) over make_tierb configs; locked grid=4,n_active=3,overlap=0.6,dim=40,n_class=4 (64 clusters) where GD-hidden genuinely RISES (0.374->0.481). Re-runs contrast w in {1,2,4} vs energy-wall vs GD ceiling on this task.

nodes.label: the depth-headroom synthetic task
nodes.description: A make_tierb configuration deliberately hunted so that GD-hidden's own per-layer probe RISES with depth (0.39->0.51) -- the necessary precondition for 'depth composes' to be a testable/falsifiable claim at all.

nodes.label: P3.2 verdict: DECISIVE PASS — given headroom, contrast+coordination COMPOSES depth
nodes.description: energy-wall declines -0.0055. contrast w1 rises-to-L3 then falls (+0.0010, myopic). w2 rises +0.0124. w4 rises MONOTONE +0.0220 (0.41->0.569 at L8, tail-retention 0.99, selectivity +0.181), exceeding the fixed-budget GD ceiling (~0.51-0.52). Monotone w1<w2<w4 in 5/5 seeds. OVERTURNS P2.2's 'intrinsic to forward-only locality.'

nodes.label: Decision: static-depth thesis proven -> carry contrast+coordination(w>=2) to P3.3 (continual veto)
nodes.description: The reframe + Direction 1 are validated on the static axis. The load-bearing claim is the monotone RISING SLOPE, not the GD-beat (GD is a fixed-budget from-scratch baseline, not a tuned max). Direct-feedback (side road B) now lower-value since the window already earns the rise. Project-deciding question moves to continual preservation.
